data.content_ref: inline:{"claim":"Prolonged megadrought between 1220‑1160 BCE triggered agricultural collapse across the Eastern Mediterranean.","mechanism":"Ecological‑economic resource depletion leading to famine, loss of state revenue, and breakdown of elite redistribution.","scope":{"covers":["Mycenaean Greece","Hittite Anatolia","Ugarit","Levantine city‑states"],"excludes":["Egypt"]},"forbidden":[{"case":"Archaeobotanical pollen data from the period showing stable moisture levels in Anatolia.","eval":"rubric:std-hist"}],"prose_notes":"Formal abstract summarizing climate proxy series and economic models."}
